prTitle: feat: implement StreakEngine domain logic and DashboardViewModel with DashboardState aggregation
prBody:
## Summary

- Add `StreakEngine` pure Swift struct in `FitnessTracker/Domain/StreakEngine.swift` with no SwiftUI or SwiftData dependencies
- `currentStreak(activityDates:today:calendar:)` — counts consecutive active days ending on today or yesterday (streak stays "live" until a 2-day gap occurs, per FR-003)
- `longestStreak(activityDates:calendar:)` — scans the full date history and returns the best consecutive-day run
- Both methods normalise inputs to calendar-day granularity and handle duplicate dates transparently
- `StreakEngineTests` covers: empty input, zero-streak (gap), single-day (today & yesterday), multi-day, gap reset, today extending an existing streak, unsorted input, duplicate deduplication, and longest-streak selection across multiple runs (20+ test cases)
- Add `DashboardState` struct that aggregates HealthKit daily statistics, today's consumed macro totals (kcal/protein/carbs/fat), and activity streak counts into a single observable value type
- Update `DashboardViewModel` to `@MainActor` with injectable `nutritionRepository` and `progressRepository` dependencies alongside the existing `healthKitService`
- Add `loadDashboard(for:)` method that loads all three data sources sequentially and populates the full `DashboardState`
- Add `DashboardViewModelTests.swift` with `MockProgressRepository`, `MockHealthKitServiceForDashboard`, and 30+ test cases covering full aggregation, partial failures, error propagation, and edge cases

Closes #30
